zone_6b_notes: Plant bare-root canes in early spring (March to April) in Farmington. Thornless varieties (Triple Crown, Chester, Apache) make harvest much easier and are reliably hardy in Zone 6b. Blackberries are a semi-erect or trailing cane fruit; needs wire trellis 5 feet high, 2–3 wires. Produces on second-year canes (floricanes) — prune out all canes that fruited immediately after harvest and keep new (primocane) growth. Missouri summers are nearly ideal for blackberry production. Triple Crown is the most reliable thornless for Zone 6 — outstanding flavor, productive, late July to August harvest.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant canes March to April; trellis required
